mnemonics.title: Widening Conversion Order
mnemonics.content: B S I L F D - Byte Short Int Long Float Double. Remember: 'Boys Should Inherit Large Fortunes During' or 'Bach Sare Inke Lenge Fate Dete Hain' (Hindi)

mnemonics.title: Unary Promotion Rule
mnemonics.content: BSC (Byte Short Char) → Int. Remember: 'Boys Start Counting' or 'Bade Saale Charsi' (informal)

mnemonics.title: Binary Promotion Rule
mnemonics.content: Smaller type promotes to larger type. Smaller loses precision to larger. Remember: 'SMALLER to LARGER, no data loss'.

mnemonics.title: Char to Int Trick
mnemonics.content: Char in arithmetic = ASCII value. Remember: 'A' for 'Arithmetic' = 65. Digit chars: '0'=48, '1'=49...

acronyms.usage: When two different numeric types in binary operation, promote smaller to larger type.
Unary operators (+, -, ~, ++, --) on byte/short/char promote to int.

visualTips.visualization: Draw pyramid: base (byte) → short → int → long → float → double at top. Show arrows pointing up meaning 'promotes to'.
Flowchart: Operand1 Type + Operand2 Type → Check which is larger → Promote smaller to larger → Result type = larger type.
Table with columns: Type, Size, Range. byte: 1B, short: 2B, int: 4B, long: 8B, float: 4B, double: 8B. Show int can hold all byte/short values.

analogies.analogy: Like pouring water from smaller glass to larger glass - no loss. But pouring from larger to smaller may overflow (potential data loss). Java prevents this automatically.
Like adding meters to centimeters - convert both to same unit first, then add. Smaller unit (cm) becomes larger (m) before operation.
Like using a calculator to add 1 to a digit - the calculator always works in its base unit (int), not the digit's type.
